title: Podman build/Windows: ADD step seems not to respect proxy settings
body:
### Issue Description

Hi,
I am struggling since a while with Podman 5.x on Windows. Basically `podman pull` / `run` work as expected, despite a PROXY & self signed certificate is in place (of course configured as documented). Also `podman build` works unless the `ADD` instruction. 

E.g. when the `ADD` command is used with a download link the proxy is bypassed on myside - resulting in a `403` errorcode.
The `RUN` commands e.g. `apt update / apt install` use the proxy as expected.

I will try to share the interesting parts as seen by the `podman-machine-default` opened up using `podman machine ssh`.
```bash
# cat /etc/systemd/system.conf.d/default-env.conf
[Manager]
DefaultEnvironment="http_proxy=http://proxy.***.com:8080"
DefaultEnvironment="https_proxy=http://proxy.***.com:8080"
DefaultEnvironment="no_proxy=localhost,127.0.0.1,.***.com"
DefaultEnvironment="HTTP_PROXY=http://proxy.***.com:8080"
DefaultEnvironment="HTTPS_PROXY=http://proxy.***.com:8080"
DefaultEnvironment="NO_PROXY=localhost,127.0.0.1,.***.com"
# export
export
declare -x DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS="unix:path=/run/user/0/bus"
declare -x DOCKER_HOST="unix:///run/podman/podman.sock"
declare -x FTP_PROXY="http://proxy.***.com:8080"
declare -x HISTCONTROL="ignoredups"
declare -x HISTSIZE="1000"
declare -x HOME="/root"
declare -x HOSTNAME="***pc2"
declare -x HTTPS_PROXY="http://proxy.***.com:8080"
declare -x HTTP_PROXY="http://proxy.***.com:8080"
declare -x LANG="C.UTF-8"
declare -x LOGNAME="root"
declare -x LS_COLORS="..."
declare -x MAIL="/var/spool/mail/root"
declare -x MOTD_SHOWN="pam"
declare -x NO_PROXY="localhost,127.0.0.1,.***.com"
declare -x OLDPWD="/root"
declare -x PATH="/root/.local/bin:/root/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin"
declare -x PWD="/mnt/c/..."
declare -x SHELL="/bin/bash"
declare -x SHLVL="1"
declare -x SSH_CLIENT="::1 40696 62788"
declare -x SSH_CONNECTION="::1 40696 ::1 62788"
declare -x SSH_TTY="/dev/pts/0"
declare -x TERM="xterm-256color"
declare -x USER="root"
declare -x XDG_RUNTIME_DIR="/run/user/0"
declare -x XDG_SESSION_CLASS="user"
declare -x XDG_SESSION_ID="38"
declare -x XDG_SESSION_TYPE="tty"
declare -x ftp_proxy="http://proxy.***.com:8080"
declare -x http_proxy="http://proxy.***.com:8080"
declare -x https_proxy="http://proxy.***.com:8080"
declare -x no_proxy="localhost,127.0.0.1,.***.com"
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Use a network with a proxy
2. Example `Dockerfile`
```Dockerfile
# FROM will download the image using the proxy
FROM docker.io/debian:bullseye-slim as builder

# RUN apt - will use the proxy for downloading
RUN apt update

# I expect the file to be downloaded - according to wireshark on Windows side the proxy is bypassed
ADD https://github.com/ncopa/su-exec/archive/dddd1567b7c76365e1e0aac561287975020a8fad.zip su-exec.zip
```
3. `podman build --tag testme --no-cache --pull=always --log-level=trace .`
4. E.g. use Wireshark to monitor the traffic - the `ADD` command tries to directly connect to `github.com` bypassing the proxy


### Describe the results you received

The final `ADD` invocation returns a `403`.

[podman5_3_2_build_log.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18659247/podman5_3_2_build_log.txt)

### Describe the results you expected

I would expect the `ADD` command downloads the referenced file using the proxy.

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
I'm having the same issues with Podman's VM (5.3.2) under macOS. Since I'm new to Podman, I don't know if it worked in a previous version.

As a workaround, I've replaced `ADD` with an appropriate `RUN curl ...` instruction.
In the meanwhile I tested various versions - the latest version which is tested fine in `4.9.5`. As far as I can say the problem showed up with `5.x`.
also seen on Fedora 41:  podman-5.3.2-1.fc41.x86_64 + podman-5.4.0-1.fc41.x86_64

lets close this issue as buildah 1.42.0 [1] is used since podman version 5.7.0 [2]

[1] https://github.com/containers/buildah/releases/tag/v1.42.0
[2] https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v5.7.0
